title: calochortus westonii
intro: Calochortus westonii, also known as Weston's mariposa lily, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Calochortus. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is known for its showy, cup-shaped flowers that bloom in shades of pink, yellow, and white. The medicinal properties of Calochortus westonii include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. Calochortus westonii can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
